adjudication_note: No new blocker was introduced. Where seats reused one ID for different effects, only the evidence-backed surviving effect is retained.

challenges_to_other_seats.challenge: Blessed/correspondence and villain failures overreach the frozen image. Selected A's bowed burden, open debt-demand hand, calcification, disgrace, and giant planted Tank read are visible.
Generic-thief-only is refuted by the half-hidden face, reward-scanning eye, adaptive smile, deferential crouch, and hand already invading the target's space.
The cost is not merely decorative: erased mouth/contours and scored identity marks visibly embody loss, while skin and blade ribbons remain matte enough against the approved anchor.

This ID duplicates emotion convergence already retained under GROUP-001 or overstates two local leather repairs as a roster-wide failure. Cashmere and Kett remain locally blocked; GROUP-002 is cleared.
The side clamp remains visible at master and 96 px, and localized organic sheen does not independently prove a hard-matte failure. The face/pose blocker remains sufficient and defensible.
The androgynous-lane/roster-collision claim is unsupported, and SABLE-002 duplicates the psychology/villain failure already retained under SABLE-001.
REDESIGN is disproportionate. Each unit retains a professional core silhouette, dominant idea, and usable role read; the surviving failures are repairable face/pose, hierarchy, or finish issues.

concessions.concession: Seat C's cue-specific reading refutes my earlier Korath psychology blocker; the frozen face and open-hand pose carry coercive martyrdom adequately.

At native face scale, elderly elf/goblin reads before batlike predator, so the bypass is unearned. The separately gripped bow also fails the declared grown spine/wing-finger correspondence. The face-contradiction portion of A's NYXA-002 is not retained.
P006 supports a local Cashmere hard-matte/material-individuality repair: broad polished black leather approaches the Vellum costume/material lane.
The role board shows a defensible repeated long split-drape/one-leg-emphasis pattern across Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna.
My separate Luna role/96 blocker was overreach; the diagonal bridge and Mage identity remain legible enough. The psychology/villain blocker survives.
My crossbow/96-role claim was too broad, but the same ID survives for the independently supported decorative-not-causal supernatural cost and weak Overload channel.
My cane/role framing was too broad. The ID survives on the shared, narrower finding that concealed fear and the service-smile contradiction are not face-readable.

protected_dissent.position: Nyxa's age bypass and body-grown-bow correspondence remain unresolved against the frozen master and therefore block READY.
Blood and enclosure do not substitute for target-directed nonhuman psychology; passive egg/armadillo remains the first read.
Cashmere's three roughs remain one upright ledger/drape envelope rather than three genuinely different silhouette hypotheses.
P003 still converges multiple humanoids into stern brows, tight mouths, smirks, or scowls; written contradiction labels cannot clear visible convergence.

group_disposition.summary: Emotion-family convergence and repeated split-drape feminine costuming survive. Roster-wide black-leather failure is cleared as duplicate/overbroad; local Cashmere and Kett material blockers remain.
